LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KARLA MORALES' MOTION REQUESTING LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF KARLA MORALES'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AND THE INTEREST IN JUDICIAL ECONOMY FAVOR GRANTING THE MOTION. THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT WOULD ADD A SINGLE CAUSE OF ACTION COMPRISING THREE NEW CHARGING ALLEGATIONS THAT SUBSTANTIALLY OVERLAP WITH PLAINTIFF'S EXISTING ALLEGATIONS AND CAUSES OF ACTIONS. DEFENDANTS' CLAIM THAT THE NEW CAUSE OF ACTION IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS MAY BE RAISED BY DEMURRER. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO SHOW THEY WOULD BE SUBSTANTIALLY PREJUDICED BY ALLOWING THE AMENDMENT. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MOVED FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ANY OF THE CLAIMS IN THE EXISTING COMPLAINT. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITIONS THAT PLAINTIFF RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS TO ANY ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY DEFENDANTS REASONABLY MAY SEEK CONCERNING THE NEWLY-ADDED CAUSE OF ACTION. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion Requesting Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KARLA MORALES' Motion Requesting Leave To Amend Complaint.

Plaintiff Karla Morales's motion for leave to amend complaint is granted. California's liberal policy in favor of allowing amendments to pleadings and the interest in judicial economy favor granting the motion. The proposed amended complaint would add a single cause of action comprising three new charging allegations that substantially overlap with Plaintiff's existing allegations and causes of actions. Defendants' claim that the new cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations may be raised by demurrer. Defendants have failed to show they would be substantially prejudiced by allowing the amendment. Defendants have not moved for summary judgment or summary adjudication of any of the claims in the existing complaint. Leave to amend is granted on the conditions that Plaintiff respond within 15 days to any additional discovery Defendants reasonably may seek concerning the newly-added cause of action.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
